items.title: OpenAI Tops $850 Billion Valuation | Bloomberg Tech 4/1/2026
items.standfirst: Bloomberg’s Tim Stenovec discusses OpenAI’s recent mega-funding round that valued the company at $852 billion. Plus, Anthropic blames the accidental release of internal source code behind its Claude coding assistant on human error. And, it’s launch day for Artemis II as NASA prepares to send astronauts back to the moon’s vicinity. (Source: Bloomberg)

items.standfirst: NASA’s Artemis II is set to launch, returning astronauts to the moon’s vicinity for the first time in 50 years. (Source: Bloomberg)

clusters.title: Elon Musk's SpaceX files confidential initial public offering
clusters.neutral_headline: Elon Musk's SpaceX files confidential initial public offering
bullet_summary: The company submitted its draft IPO registration to the US Securities and Exchange Commission, the people said, asking not to be identified as the information isn’t public
75T, people familiar with the matter have said

items.title: Elon Musk's SpaceX files confidential initial public offering
items.standfirst: The Texas-based company could go public with a valuation of more than $1.75 trillion, making it the largest IPO in history.
items.content: The Texas-based company could go public with a valuation of more than $1.75 trillion, making it the largest IPO in history.

items.title: SpaceX Said to File Confidentially for IPO Before AI Rivals
items.standfirst: SpaceX has filed confidentially for an initial public offering, according to people familiar with the matter, bringing billionaire Elon Musk’s rocket, satellite and AI company closer to delivering the biggest-ever listing. The company submitted its draft IPO registration to the US Securities and Exchange Commission, the people said, asking not to be identified as the information isn’t public. A representative for SpaceX didn’t immediately respond to a request for comment. SpaceX could seek a valuation in the IPO of more than $1.75T, people familiar with the matter have said. The company acquired Musk’s artificial intelligence startup xAI in a deal that valued the enlarged entity at $1.25T. Bloomberg US Equity Capital Markets Reporter Anthony Hughes joins Paul Sweeney and Scarlet Fu on Bloomberg Intelligence to discuss. (Source: Bloomberg)
items.content: SpaceX has filed confidentially for an initial public offering, according to people familiar with the matter, bringing billionaire Elon Musk’s rocket, satellite and AI company closer to delivering the biggest-ever listing. The company submitted its draft IPO registration to the US Securities and Exchange Commission, the people said, asking not to be identified as the information isn’t public. A representative for SpaceX didn’t immediately respond to a request for comment. SpaceX could seek a valuation in the IPO of more than $1.75T, people familiar with the matter have said. The company acquired Musk’s artificial intelligence startup xAI in a deal that valued the enlarged entity at $1.25T. Bloomberg US Equity Capital Markets Reporter Anthony Hughes joins Paul Sweeney and Scarlet Fu on Bloomberg Intelligence to discuss. (Source: Bloomberg)

items.title: SpaceX confidentially files to go public at $1.75tn, reports say
items.standfirst: Elon Musk’s rocket company could go public as early as June, Bloomberg reportsSign up for the Breaking News US email to get newsletter alerts in your inboxSpaceX has confidentially filed for an initial public offering on the US stock market, according to reports from Bloomberg and the Wall Street Journal. The IPO is set to be one of the most closely watched and highly valued listings in market history.Elon Musk’s company, which has become a dominant power in both space travel and satellite communications, could potentially seek a valuation upwards of $1.75tn. The confidential filing will give regulators a period to review and discuss the company’s financial disclosures before investors and the public are able to view them. Continue reading...

items.title: US approves new oral weight-loss pill developed by Eli Lilly
items.standfirst: Called Foundayo, the tablet becomes the second one to receive FDA’s green light after Novo Nordisk’s WegovyThe US Food and Drug Administration (FDA) gave the green light on Wednesday to a new oral weight-loss medication developed by the Indianapolis-based pharmaceutical company Eli Lilly.Known as orforglipron or brand name Foundayo, the once-daily tablet becomes the second GLP-1 drug in pill form to hit the market in a short span of time, arriving after Novo Nordisk’s Wegovy pill received approval in December. Continue reading...

items.title: Polls show consistent majorities opposing military action in Iran after a month of war
items.standfirst: When President Donald Trump provides an "important update" on the Iran war Wednesday night, he'll be addressing a nation that has been deeply skeptical of the military operation since it began.
items.content: When President Donald Trump provides an "important update" on the Iran war Wednesday night, he'll be addressing a nation that has been deeply skeptical of the military operation since it began.

items.title: UK PM calls NATO world's 'most effective military alliance' after Trump criticism
items.standfirst: British Prime Minister Keir Starmer called NATO "the single most effective military alliance the world has ever seen" Wednesday following renewed criticism of the group by US President Donald Trump. "It has kept us safe for many decades, and we are fully committed to NATO," Starmer told a press conference, after Trump told the UK's Telegraph newspaper that the alliance was a "paper tiger". FRANCE 24's International Affairs Editor Douglas Herbert tells us more.
items.content: British Prime Minister Keir Starmer called NATO "the single most effective military alliance the world has ever seen" Wednesday following renewed criticism of the group by US President Donald Trump. "It has kept us safe for many decades, and we are fully committed to NATO," Starmer told a press conference, after Trump told the UK's Telegraph newspaper that the alliance was a "paper tiger". FRANCE 24's International Affairs Editor Douglas Herbert tells us more.

items.title: US Trump says will be leaving Iran 'very soon'
items.standfirst: US President Donald Trump said the military could end its Iran offensive in two to three weeks and will shift responsibility for the Strait of Hormuz to countries that rely on it for oil and shipping as the White House announced a prime-time presidential address Wednesday evening on the war. FRANCE 24's International Affairs Commentator Douglas Herbert tells us more.
items.content: US President Donald Trump said the military could end its Iran offensive in two to three weeks and will shift responsibility for the Strait of Hormuz to countries that rely on it for oil and shipping as the White House announced a prime-time presidential address Wednesday evening on the war. FRANCE 24's International Affairs Commentator Douglas Herbert tells us more.

items.title: Trump claims Iran wants a ceasefire, Iran says remarks are ‘false and baseless’
items.standfirst: Donald Trump has claimed that Iran has asked the United States for a ceasefire -- adding that Washington would only consider it when the Strait of Hormuz reopens. Tehran, though, has denied this -- the Foreign Ministry calling Trump's remarks "baseless." It comes hours ahead of the president's primetime address to the nation. FRANCE 24's Carys Garland reports.
items.content: Donald Trump has claimed that Iran has asked the United States for a ceasefire -- adding that Washington would only consider it when the Strait of Hormuz reopens. Tehran, though, has denied this -- the Foreign Ministry calling Trump's remarks "baseless." It comes hours ahead of the president's primetime address to the nation. FRANCE 24's Carys Garland reports.

clusters.neutral_headline: Judge rules Trump cutting off funding for NPR and PBS is unconstitutional
bullet_summary: Reported by CBS News, Guardian Business

items.title: Judge rules Trump cutting off funding for NPR and PBS is unconstitutional
items.standfirst: U.S. District Judge Randolph Moss blocked the Trump administration from enforcing provisions of his executive order that directed federal agencies to cut off funding to NPR and PBS.
items.content: U.S. District Judge Randolph Moss blocked the Trump administration from enforcing provisions of his executive order that directed federal agencies to cut off funding to NPR and PBS.

items.title: Federal judge blocks Trump order to end funding for NPR and PBS
items.standfirst: Citing the first amendment, judge says president’s executive order is unlawful and unenforceableSign up for the Breaking News US email to get newsletter alerts in your inboxCiting the first amendment, a federal judge on Tuesday agreed to permanently block the Trump administration from implementing a presidential directive to end federal funding for National Public Radio (NPR) and the Public Broadcasting Service (PBS), two media entities that the White House has said are counterproductive to American priorities.The operational impact of US district judge Randolph Moss’s decision was not immediately clear – both because it will probably be appealed and because too much damage to the public-broadcasting system has already been done, both by the president and Congress. Continue reading...
items.content: Citing the first amendment, judge says president’s executive order is unlawful and unenforceableSign up for the Breaking News US email to get newsletter alerts in your inboxCiting the first amendment, a federal judge on Tuesday agreed to permanently block the Trump administration from implementing a presidential directive to end federal funding for National Public Radio (NPR) and the Public Broadcasting Service (PBS), two media entities that the White House has said are counterproductive to American priorities.The operational impact of US district judge Randolph Moss’s decision was not immediately clear – both because it will probably be appealed and because too much damage to the public-broadcasting system has already been done, both by the president and Congress. Continue reading...

items.standfirst: Crown Prosecution Service confirms support on inquiries after arrests on suspicion of misconduct in public officePolice are receiving advice from prosecutors as part of their inquiries into Peter Mandelson and Andrew Mountbatten-Windsor’s links to Jeffrey Epstein.The former duke of York and the former UK ambassador to the US were both arrested in February on suspicion of misconduct in public office over their connections with the late financier.
